insight:
Yesterday was clearly a rest day with just 3,467 steps and no formal training activities. Your body seems to have used this downtime well - you got an excellent 10.3 hours of sleep, your resting heart rate of 48 bpm shows solid cardiovascular fitness, and stress levels were low at 27/100. The 2,272 calories burned aligns nicely with a recovery day.

Your HRV at 91ms sits right at the bottom of your baseline range (91-113ms), which isn't alarming but suggests your body is still processing recent training stress. Combined with the low activity yesterday, this indicates you're ready for a moderate training session today rather than anything too intense. With 11 days until Chester, stick to your planned workout but listen to your body.

Given yesterday's low activity and approaching race day, focus on getting your full 175g protein target today to support muscle recovery and adaptation. Your metabolism might be slightly slower after the rest day, so be mindful of portion sizes to stay on track with your 2,300 calorie goal.
